scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty Five Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from date fixed for performance, or if no date fixed, when plaintiff has notice that performance is refused. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into an Agreement to Sell for the purchase of a residential property owned by the Defendant. The terms of the agreement were reduced into writing and signed by both parties, a copy of which is annexed hereto as **Annexure A**.

6. The property agreed to be sold is Flat No. 302, situated on the 3rd Floor of Sunshine Apartments, located at Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft. The specific survey number of the land is {{PROPERTY_SURVEY_NUMBER}}. The total sale consideration agreed upon between the parties is Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty Five Lakhs only).

7. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement to Sell, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Five Lakhs only) to the Defendant on the date of the agreement itself. The payment was made via RTGS, and the transaction bears UTR Number HDFC20240110987654. Proof of the said payment is annexed hereto as **Annexure B**.

8. The remaining balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- (Rupees Sixty Lakhs only) was agreed to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024, against the execution and registration of the final Sale Deed. The Plaintiff has maintained sufficient funds to discharge this obligation within the stipulated timeline.

9. At all times material to this suit, the Plaintiff has been ready and willing to perform her part of the contract, including the payment of the balance consideration and the execution of the Sale Deed as per the terms agreed upon on 10.01.2024.

BREACH OF CONTRACT

10. On 05.06.2024, prior to the deadline for the completion of the sale, the Defendant issued a letter addressed to the Plaintiff expressly refusing to execute the Sale Deed. A copy of the Defendant's refusal letter is annexed hereto as **Annexure C**.

11. In the said letter dated 05.06.2024, the Defendant stated that he was reneging on the Agreement to Sell because he had received {{HIGHER_OFFER_DETAILS}} from a third party. The Defendant's action constitutes a clear breach of the contractual obligations undertaken on 10.01.2024.

12. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to honour the contract and execute the Sale Deed. A copy of the legal notice along with the postal receipt is annexed hereto as **Annexure D**. The Defendant has failed to comply with the notice and continues to refuse to perform his part of the contract.

DAMAGES

13. Due to the Defendant's wrongful refusal to execute the Sale Deed, the Plaintiff has suffered prejudice and financial uncertainty, as the property market values are subject to fluctuation. The Plaintiff was deprived of the possession and enjoyment of the property scheduled to be delivered upon execution of the Sale Deed.

14. The Plaintiff continues to remain ready and willing to pay the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- and to complete the transaction pursuant to the Agreement to Sell. The Plaintiff seeks the enforcement of the contract to prevent further loss and injustice resulting from the Defendant's breach.

LEGAL BASIS

15. The Plaintiff is entitled to a decree of specific performance of the agreement under Sections 10 and 14 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, the subject matter being such that the grant of compensation in money would not afford adequate relief.

16. The Plaintiff has been ready and willing to perform his/her part of the agreement at all material times within the meaning of Section 16(c) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

17. The present suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

18. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

19. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 20.06.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

20. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

21. The suit is valued at Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty Five Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

22. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree of specific performance directing the Defendant to perform and complete the agreement dated 10.01.2024 in favour of the Plaintiff;
    (b) Direct the Defendant to execute and register the necessary conveyance / sale deed in favour of the Plaintiff upon receipt of the balance consideration;
    (c) In the alternative, award compensation / damages in lieu of specific performance;
    (d) Award costs of the suit;
    (e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654) for advance payment

Annexure C — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

Annexure D — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, Anita Sharma, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 22 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

D

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT BENGALURU

Commercial Suit No. __________ / 20____

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A company registered under the Companies Act, 2013,
Having its registered office at: MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director Priya Menon.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A Limited Liability Partnership registered under the LLP Act, 2008,
Having its office at: Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

2. The suit is valued above the specified value of Rs. 3,00,000/- as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

JURISDICTION

3. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

4. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business within such territorial limits.

5. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court. This Hon'ble Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain commercial disputes under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

6. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from when the contract is broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

7. The Plaintiff has complied with the mandatory pre-institution mediation requirement under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, [OR: The Plaintiff seeks urgent interim relief and is exempted from pre-institution mediation under Section 12A]. A copy of the mediation certificate [OR: exemption affidavit] is annexed herewith.

FACTS OF THE CASE

8. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff entered into a Software Development Agreement with the Defendant for the development of an ERP system, with a total contract value of Rs. 45,00,000/-. A copy of the said Agreement is annexed herewith as Annexure A.

9. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- as the first milestone payment to the Defendant via NEFT on 01.02.2024, bearing UTR Number ICIC20240201456789. A copy of the NEFT receipt is annexed herewith as Annexure B.

10. As per the agreed timeline, the Defendant was obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system by 01.05.2024.

BREACH PARTICULARS

11. The Defendant failed to deliver the first module or any part of the software by the stipulated deadline of 01.05.2024.

12. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant communicated its abandonment of the project without completing the deliverables, as evidenced by the email correspondence between the parties. A copy of the said email correspondence is annexed herewith as Annexure C.

13. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 demanding refund and performance, which remains uncomplied with, and the Defendant has not returned the advance payment. A copy of the legal notice is annexed herewith as Annexure E.

DAMAGES

14. The Plaintiff is entitled to the refund of the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- retained by the Defendant without performance.

15. Due to the non-delivery and delay in operations, the Plaintiff suffered consequential losses quantified at Rs. 12,00,000/-, as assessed in the loss assessment report prepared by the auditor. A copy of the said report is annexed herewith as Annexure D.

16. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 18% per annum on the principal amount and damages from the date of breach until realization.

LEGAL BASIS

17. The Defendant has committed a breach of the agreement by failing to perform the obligations undertaken therein, thereby entitling the Plaintiff to claim damages under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

18. The Defendant has committed a repudiatory breach by refusing to perform its obligations, entitling the Plaintiff to treat the contract as at an end and seek appropriate relief.

19. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.

20. The present suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

21. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

22. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 01.07.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

23. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

24. The suit is valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

25. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:
    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:
    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only) towards damages as particularised in the Schedule of Damages annexed hereto;
    (b) Award pendente lite and future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (c) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
    (d) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

(i) Actual Loss (Refund of Advance)
     Amount claimed: Rs. 20,00,000/-
     Basis / methodology: Non-performance of contract
     Documentary proof: Annexure B (NEFT Receipt)

(ii) Consequential loss (within the contemplation of the parties at the time of contract, per Section 73 second limb / Hadley v. Baxendale)
     Amount claimed: Rs. 12,00,000/-
     Basis / methodology: Operational delay due to non-delivery
     Documentary proof: Annexure D (Loss Assessment Report)

TOTAL DAMAGES CLAIMED: Rs. 32,00,000/-

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024
Annexure B — NEFT r
